spec_match.rationale: Uses `content: '...' / counter(name)` — the exact spec syntax to put counter() in the alt-text segment of the content property.

interactive.rationale: Toggle between old/new behaviour with a simulated a11y readout — past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggle between old and new behaviour with what AT announces; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't cover `counters()` (the plural with separator), nor what happens when counter() in alt fails (missing scope, undefined counter).

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5185442420621312 link in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Show counters() (plural) too
openQuestions.detail: The feature name is 'counter() AND counters()'. Currently only counter() is demonstrated; add a counters() example with a separator to cover the full spec change.

openQuestions.title: Test undefined counter in alt
openQuestions.detail: What does AT announce if counter(missingName) is in the alt slot? Empty string? The literal name? Demonstrate so authors know the failure mode.

summary: Captures the core spec change but stops at counter(); the page should also show counters() to match the feature name.
